Class Croissant

java.lang.Object
Main.ObjectInGame.Items.BaseItem
Main.ObjectInGame.Items.Croissant
All Implemented Interfaces:
Interactable
public class Croissant extends BaseItem
Represents a Croissant item that can move with physics and provide different effects depending on its type when interacted with. The croissant moves using simple gravity physics and can bounce once when hitting the ground.

    Indicates whether the croissant has already bounced.

  • Field Details

    • vy

      public double vy
      Vertical velocity of the croissant.

    • hasBounced

      public boolean hasBounced
      Indicates whether the croissant has already bounced.

  • Constructor Details

    • Croissant

      public Croissant(CroissantType type, double vx)
      Creates a croissant with a specified type and horizontal velocity. The image name is determined based on the croissant type.
      Parameters:
      type - the type of croissant
      vx - the initial horizontal velocity

  • Method Details

    • updatePhysics

      public void updatePhysics(double dt, ItemView view, double groundY)
      Updates the croissant's physics including gravity, movement, and ground collision handling. The croissant falls with gravity, moves horizontally while the player is alive, and bounces once when touching the ground.
      Parameters:
      dt - time delta used for physics calculations
      view - the visual representation of the croissant
      groundY - the Y position of the ground

    • interact

      public void interact(Cookie player)
      Applies the croissant's effect when the player interacts with it. The effect depends on the croissant type: - ORIGINAL: adds a large amount of score - BUTTER: adds score and activates a ChillyBoost - STRAWBERRY: heals the player and adds score
      Parameters:
      player - the Cookie that interacts with the croissant

    • setSpeed

      public void setSpeed(double vx, double vy)
      Sets the velocity of the croissant.
      Parameters:
      vx - the horizontal velocity
      vy - the vertical velocity
